content.problemStatement: Given the root of a Binary Tree, return the height of the tree. The height of the tree is equal to the number of nodes on the longest path from root to a leaf.
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the tree <= 10^4
content.inputFormat: The input is the root of a binary tree.
content.outputFormat: The output is the height of the binary tree.

solution.spaceComplexity: O(H) for recursive, O(N) for iterative

code.Python3:
from queue import Queue

class Node:
    def __init__(self, val):
        self.data = val
        self.left = None
        self.right = None

class Solution:
    def maxDepth(self, root):
        if root is None:
            return 0
        lh = self.maxDepth(root.left)
        rh = self.maxDepth(root.right)
        return 1 + max(lh, rh)

# Creating a sample binary tree
root = Node(1)
root.left = Node(2)
root.right = Node(3)
root.left.left = Node(4)
root.left.right = Node(5)
root.left.right.right = Node(6)
root.left.right.right.right = Node(7)

solution = Solution()
depth = solution.maxDepth(root)
print("Maximum depth of the binary tree:", depth)
